results.title: Could the SEC really break away? Commish says conversations are real but that's not what they want
results.description: Southeastern Conference Commissioner Greg Sankey says the SEC is having serious talks about breaking away from the NCAA. On Monday, Sankey explained that school presidents are frustrated and considering significant changes. However, he emphasized that this isn't their preferred solution....

results.title: Halpin and Hoskins hit 2 homers each and Guardians hit 7 longballs in win over Twins
results.description: CLEVELAND — Petey Halpin and Rhys Hoskins each went deep twice and the Cleveland Guardians matched a franchise record for a home game by hitting seven home runs in their 13-4 victory over the Minnesota Twins on Monday night.

results.title: Dodgers say two-way star Shohei Ohtani is expected to pitch off a mound later this week
results.description: Los Angeles Dodgers two-way star Shohei Ohtani is expected to pitch off a mound sometime later this week, but there is no timetable for his return to the rotation. The four-time MVP last pitched on July 3. He received a...

results.title: Mike Phillips now officially with Japan B.League's Kyoto Hannaryz
results.description: Mike Phillips is officially headed to the Japan B. League, signing a two-year deal with the Kyoto Hannaryz starting in the 2026-2027 season this September.

results.title: Liverpool can only make ONE specific signing thanks to Premier League rule
results.description: Liverpool could find it a tad tough to make new signings in the window this summer

results.title: Washington State lands first Men's Basketball commitment in 2027 class
results.description: PULLMAN, Wash. - Washington State men’s basketball has picked up its first commitment for the 2027 recruiting cycle, as three-star guard Jaylen McLaughlin announced his pledge to the Cougars on Monday.

results.title: LOCAL ROUNDUP: Illini tickets to go on sale
results.description: CHAMPAIGN — The University of Illinois will start selling single-game tickets at noon on Thursday.
